title: Economic Conditions, Risks and Monetary Policy
summary: Musalem judges current policy as modestly restrictive and roughly appropriate, noting multiple Taylor-rule specifications prescribe a rate around today's funds level and above neutral. He sees the labor market near full employment but with downside risks that have recently risen, while core inflation runs closer to 3% than 2% on tariff effects he expects to fade over the next two to three quarters. He stresses a balanced approach: warning that putting too much weight on the labor goal risks excessive easing, a steeper yield curve, higher term premium and un-anchored inflation expectations, while too much weight on inflation risks under-supporting employment. The net message is cautious and balanced, leaning hold, with concern that the public and markets remain sensitive to inflation upside.

key_quotes.quote: The current modestly restrictive setting of the policy rate is consistent with today's full employment labor market and core inflation nearly one percentage point above the Fed's 2% target.
key_quotes.context: Monetary Policy section, his read of the current stance

key_quotes.quote: Multiple Taylor rule specifications currently prescribe a policy rate around today's level of the federal funds rate and above estimates of the long-run neutral rate.
key_quotes.context: Monetary Policy section, justifying holding near current rate

key_quotes.quote: putting most of the weight on the labor market goal runs a risk of unwarranted or excessive policy easing, causing a further steepening of the yield curve, a rise in the term premium or an increase in inflation expectations.
key_quotes.context: Monetary Policy section, cautioning against aggressive cuts

key_quotes.quote: Recent data have further increased my perception of downside risks to the labor market.
key_quotes.context: Labor Market section, listing longer-term unemployment, cyclically sensitive groups, payroll revisions
